skill.name: repo-intake-and-plan
skill.description: Scans an AI repository, reads README and common project files, extracts documented commands, classifies likely reproduction paths, and returns a conservative planning summary.
skill.summary: Create a README-first reproduction plan for AI repositories.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported five high-risk weak-crypto hits and twenty-four command-related hits. Manual review found no cryptographic code, shell execution, network access, or prompt injection attempt; the hits are Markdown backticks, regex parsing, language labels, and argparse descriptions. Residual risk is low because helper scripts read local repository files and extract untrusted README commands for planning.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines contain skill descriptions or argparse help text, not cryptographic operations. No weak hash, cipher use, key handling, or secret processing was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines were inspected and do not contain cryptographic APIs or algorithms. The matches are caused by ordinary words inside descriptions.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified As Command Execution
low_findings.description: The cited reference lines are Markdown file names, directory names, and classification labels. They document scan priorities and do not invoke Ruby, shell, or any external process.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These locations are Markdown documentation, not executable Ruby or shell code. The backticks delimit inline code labels only.

low_findings.title: README Command Extraction Requires Human Review Before Execution
low_findings.description: The helper script extracts command text from README code blocks and inline prompts, including PowerShell-labeled blocks. It does not execute the commands, but downstream agents should not run extracted commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly parses and prints command strings without subprocess calls. The remaining risk is semantic because README commands are untrusted user-controlled content.

low_findings.title: Repository Scanner Reads Local Paths
low_findings.description: The scanner accepts a repository path, lists top-level files, and returns resolved local paths in its output. This is expected for the skill, but users should run it only on intended repositories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem reads are explicit and limited to a user-supplied repository root. No network transmission or hidden exfiltration path was found.

content.value_statement: AI research repositories often hide the safest reproduction path among many setup, training, and evaluation commands. This skill reads the repository first and produces a conservative plan before execution begins.
actual_capabilities: Reads README files and common project files for reproduction signals.
Extracts shell-like commands from fenced blocks and inline prompts.
Classifies commands as inference, evaluation, training, setup, asset, smoke, or other work.
Summarizes top-level repository structure and missing key files.
Highlights ambiguity so the main agent can avoid unsupported assumptions.
Supports conservative selection of the smallest trustworthy reproduction target.
limitations: It does not install environments, download assets, or execute reproduction commands.
It relies on README and common project files, so hidden documentation may be missed.
Command classification is heuristic and should be reviewed before use.
It does not verify paper claims or compare results against benchmarks.

use_cases.title: Start an AI Paper Reproduction
use_cases.description: Inspect a new research repository and identify the first reliable command path before setup begins.

use_cases.title: Review Repository Reproducibility
use_cases.description: Summarize documented commands, missing files, and unclear setup requirements for an incoming model repository.

use_cases.title: Triage Training and Evaluation Paths
use_cases.description: Separate likely inference, evaluation, and training commands so expensive workflows are not selected by mistake.

prompt_templates.title: Scan a Repository
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner intake for a local repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan this repository and summarize the README, key project files, documented commands, and the safest first reproduction target.

prompt_templates.title: Classify Documented Commands
prompt_templates.scenario: Organize README commands before execution planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the README and classify each documented command as setup, asset, inference, evaluation, training, smoke, or other. Mark uncertain classifications.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Smallest Trustworthy Target
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect the repository and recommend the smallest trustworthy reproduction target. Prefer explicit README evidence and explain any ambiguity.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare an Orchestrator Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a larger reproduction workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise handoff for the main orchestrator with repository structure, command inventory, inferred paths, risks, and next safe actions.

output_examples.input: Scan a model repository before running any commands.
output_examples.output: The report identifies the README, environment files, demo command, evaluation script, and missing checkpoint instructions. It recommends a help or demo command first.

output_examples.input: Classify all commands from the README.
output: Setup commands are separated from asset downloads.
Inference commands are marked as first candidates.
Training commands are flagged as expensive and not first-choice.

output_examples.input: Prepare a handoff for reproduction planning.
output_examples.output: The handoff lists repository structure, command categories, confidence notes, and unresolved risks for the orchestrating agent.

best_practices: Use it before installing dependencies or running repository commands.
Prefer explicit README evidence over filename-based assumptions.
Review extracted commands manually before any execution step.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as an execution tool for setup, downloads, training, or evaluation.
Do not treat heuristic command categories as verified project intent.
Do not use it as a substitute for paper review or result validation.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands from the repository?
faq.answer: No. It scans files, extracts documented commands, and supports planning only.

faq.question: What repositories is it designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for AI and machine learning repositories where README-first reproduction planning is useful.

faq.question: Can it choose between inference, evaluation, and training workflows?
faq.answer: It can classify likely workflows and recommend a conservative first target, but users should review the evidence.

faq.question: Does it download checkpoints or datasets?
faq.answer: No. It may identify asset-related commands, but it does not download or prepare assets.

faq.question: Can it work without a README?
faq.answer: It can report missing README evidence and inspect common files, but the plan will be less certain.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: It does not execute repository commands, but it reads local files. Use it only on repositories you intend to inspect.
